text: Bankalar, talep üzerine ve kısa vadeli borçlanarak (mevduat kabulü, banknot ihracı) uzun vadeli krediler sağlar; bu 'vade dönüşümü' olarak bilinir ve bankaların nakit rezervleri, pazarlanabilir menkul kıymet yatırımları ve çeşitli fonlama kaynakları ile yönetilir.
validation_reason: The chunk explicitly states: 'Asset–liability mismatch/Maturity transformation – banks borrow more on demand debt and short term debt, but provide more long-term loans. In other words, they borrow short and lend long. With a stronger credit quality than most other borrowers, banks can do this by aggregating issues (e.g. accepting deposits and issuing banknotes) and redemptions (e.g. withdrawals and redemption of banknotes), maintaining reserves of cash, investing in marketable securities that can be readily converted to cash if needed, and raising replacement funding as needed from various sources (e.g. wholesale cash markets and securities markets).'
storage_reason: This nugget explains the crucial concept of maturity transformation in banking, detailing how banks manage the mismatch between short-term liabilities and long-term assets.
